name: Epilepsy - Antiepileptic Drug (AED) Query
description: Tests ability to find epilepsy patients via antiepileptic drugs (AEDs). Standard AEDs across multiple drug classes: phenytoin (sodium channel blocker), carbamazepine (sodium channel), levetiracetam (SV2A modulator — most common modern AED), valproate (broad-spectrum), lamotrigine (sodium channel + glutamate). Cross-indication exists: valproate also for bipolar/migraine, carbamazepine for trigeminal neuralgia.
prompt: Find patients prescribed antiepileptic medications including phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, or lamotrigine.

prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed antiepileptic drugs (AEDs) across multiple drug classes — sodium channel blockers (phenytoin, carbamazepine, lamotrigine), SV2A modulators (levetiracetam), and broad-spectrum AEDs (valproate).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any AED. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 8183 (phenytoin), 2002 (carbamazepine), 114477 (levetiracetam — most prescribed modern AED), 40254 (valproate/valproic acid), 28439 (lamotrigine). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. Note valproate has cross-indication with bipolar disorder and migraine; carbamazepine with trigeminal neuralgia.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with AED orders. Multiple drug classes; some cross-indication (valproate, carbamazepine).
